name: Léopoldville
notes: Urban district created around the capital city. New in the 1926 reorganization.

name: Bas-Congo
notes: Coastal district at the mouth of the Congo River. Included the ports of Boma and Matadi.

name: Kwango
notes: Named after the Kwango River. Bordered Portuguese Angola to the south.

name: Lac Léopold II
notes: Named after Lake Léopold II (now Lake Mai-Ndombe). Absorbed parts of the former Moyen-Congo district.

name: Sankuru
notes: Named after the Sankuru River. Part of the Kasai region.

name: Kasai
notes: Named after the Kasai River. Diamond-rich region.

name: Congo-Ubangi
notes: Formed from the merger of former Bangala, Ubangi, and Lulonga districts in the 1933 reorganization.

name: Tshuapa
notes: Named after the Tshuapa River. Located in the central Congo basin rainforest.

name: Stanleyville
notes: District centered on Stanleyville (now Kisangani), at the navigable head of the Congo River.

name: Uele
notes: Reconsolidated from former Bas-Uele and Haut-Uele districts. Named after the Uele River.

name: Kibali-Ituri
notes: Formed from former Ituri and parts of Aruwimi and Haut-Uele districts. Named after the Kibali and Ituri rivers. Gold mining region.

name: Kivu
notes: Named after Lake Kivu. Eastern border district with stunning highland scenery. Later became the provinces of Nord-Kivu and Sud-Kivu.

name: Maniema
notes: Formed from parts of the former Lowa and Maniema districts. Historically associated with Arab-Swahili slave trade routes.

name: Tanganika
notes: Named after Lake Tanganyika. Formed from the consolidation of Tanganika-Moero and parts of Lomami.

name: Lualaba
notes: Named after the Lualaba River (upper Congo). Formed from parts of the former Haut-Luapula and Lulua districts.

name: Haut-Katanga
notes: Upper Katanga, centered on the copper belt around Élisabethville (now Lubumbashi). The economic powerhouse of the Belgian Congo.
